creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: AI Engineer announces AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expanded content, expo floor, research-industry synergy, leadership tracks, and AI verticals. The event offers $40k in sponsor offers and YouTube discount code YOUTUBEPROMO for attendees.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents a production AI framework addressing enterprise agent deployment challenges. The "five pillars" approach integrates evaluation, observability, data foundations, multi-agent orchestration, and governance based on a case study where a retail bank's chatbot PoC failed until systematic measurement and tracing infrastructure identified root causes of production failures.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how agents frequently fail to access the open web, falling back to stale training data while falsely claiming successful searches. The session compares blocked versus unblocked agent runs across major platforms and explains the technical barriers including anti-bot systems, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA challenges that undermine agent reliability and citation accuracy.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA reduced diffusion model inference from 50 steps to 1-8 steps by combining dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation. The approach uses trajectory-based or distribution-based distillation where a student model learns to match a teacher's output in far fewer denoising steps. NVIDIA's FastGen framework packages these techniques for production use.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Top AI engineers use self-prompting loops instead of manual prompting, where agents prompt agents via an orchestrator that spawns parallel workers. This scales agent capability but requires cost control and hallucination mitigation through observability dashboards.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding models like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers. It enables workflows where different models handle different tasks such as implementation and review, with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device session persistence.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the suspension of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 following a US government export-control directive, reportedly triggered by a jailbreak claim. He contrasts pre-suspension benchmarks of Fable 5 against Opus 4.8 and discusses the implications for developers building on large language models.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides agent identity management, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade AI agents. The tool integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude and supports rapid development and deployment workflows.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer as a framework or tool for constructing AI agents. The video includes a free course on building AI agents, code templates on GitHub, and a workshop in the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive agents and self-improving company systems through AI loops. Covers technical architectures, autonomous use cases like ads optimization and SEO, and tools like Loopany that enable agent memory and tool use.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses upcoming autonomous coding tools and agent frameworks, featuring demonstrations of the /goal feature for task orchestration and /Mission exploration for multi-step workflows. The video positions these capabilities as advances toward true autonomous coder systems.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenAI Symphony as a new AI coding paradigm. The video covers what Symphony is, how it works, harness setup with auto testing, and workflow configuration for developers.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Andrej Karpathy's explanation of bigram language models, a simple but instructive approach that demonstrates tokenization, embedding, training, loss computation, and backpropagation. The video frames bigrams as essential preparation for understanding GPT and attention mechanisms in large language models.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer, Mixture of Experts (latent), and Multi-token prediction approaches.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a model using Sparse Attention instead of full attention mechanisms. The M3 includes tiled optimizations and KV cache improvements that reduce memory bandwidth during prefill and decode stages through continuous sequential reads.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agentic framework that provides a harness layer for composing and deploying AI agents. The video compares Pi against other agent platforms like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, discussing how it handles increasing complexity in agent composition.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a tool that equips AI coding agents with up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and guardrails to improve their effectiveness and reduce errors when interacting with AWS services. It bridges the gap between agents' general knowledge and current AWS capabilities.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates how to run Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock, enabling developers to keep data within AWS infrastructure for compliance and residency requirements. The walkthrough covers setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and content governance guardrails.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses career strategies for tech workers in 2026 as AI screening reshapes hiring. The video argues that personal networking, building a brand, and continuous learning remain critical for junior developers, and that AI adoption will evolve jobs rather than eliminate them. Practical advice includes open source contribution, building portfolio projects, and the Feynman Principle for mastering concepts.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production best practices for deploying AI agents built with TypeScript: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication using API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets via lifecycle hooks and model routing, and observability through metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The recommendations are demonstrated using Strands Agents SDK and AWS services.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video essay examining whether Google engineers use generative code tools. The discussion compares OpenAI Codex and Claude for code generation, surveys current practices at Google, and speculates on future adoption trends through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses the impact of Malus, a company or product, on the open source ecosystem. The video explores questions about open source maintenance sustainability and whether Malus represents a threat to open source development models.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov argues that software competitive advantage depends on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that the fear of AI destroying SaaS is exaggerated and that the best AI products create strong customer loyalty rather than simply optimizing for happiness.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the humanoid robotics focus led by companies like 1X and Unitree represents a limited vision. She advocates for robot design prioritizing human interaction and delight over pure utility as the robotics industry scales to billions of units.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy enterprise software by consolidating finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She argues that even innovative giants like Google rely on outdated tools like spreadsheets and that building a full enterprise platform from day one, combined with customer trust and selective hiring, creates sustainable competitive advantage over established 30-year-old competitors.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to distinguish real product-market fit from false signals. He argues that passionate users alone are not proof of PMF, that genuine fit manifests as customer pull rather than founder push, and that deep embedding with customers beats formal interviews for understanding product viability.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Agents: Stop Button Building, Start Startup Speed! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that building AI agents requires agility and startup-style iteration rather than traditional software engineering approaches. He contends that startups like Anthropic adapt faster to the unpredictable nature of AI agents than large technology companies.

videos.title: Microsoft & Nvidia Laptop: Local AI Revolutionized! #shorts
videos.summary: Microsoft integrates Nvidia's chip technology into laptops to enable on-device AI acceleration. This represents a hardware-level approach to local AI processing power.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
